title: Plush Silky Greek Pork Belly Gyro
description: Indulge in the rich flavors of Greece with this winey and silky pork belly gyro, featuring tender slices of pork belly, all wrapped in a warm pita bread. This dish is not only delicious but also anti-inflammatory friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together red wine, olive oil, garlic, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to create the marinade.
Add the pork belly to the marinade and let it sit at room temperature for 2 hours.
Remove the pork belly from the marinade and place it on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet.
Let the pork belly air dry in the refrigerator for 24 hours to create a dry brine.
After the dry brine, roast the pork belly in the preheated oven for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the pork belly rest for 30 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it in the oven for 5 minutes.
Assemble the gyro by placing the sliced pork belly into the warm pita bread and serving immediately.
